label: Offensive Developer
description: Develops offensive tooling, implants, loaders, and evasion code for red team operations. Focuses on Windows internals, process injection, AV/EDR bypass, and custom C2 capability development.

paths.name: Standard Track
paths.rationale: ODPC is the only performance-based offensive development cert available — 48-hour practical + report covering C/Go implant development, AMSI/ETW bypass, and EDR evasion. Paired with MalDev Academy for breadth and OSED for OffSec brand recognition. This stack proves both practical tooling ability and formal credentialing.
pros: ODPC is the only hands-on offensive dev cert — 48hr practical + professional report
OSED adds OffSec brand credibility valued by enterprise and federal employers
MalDev Academy is the community-recommended learning platform — used by SpecterOps for onboarding
All practical — no MCQ credentials in the stack
cons: Requires strong Red Team Operator foundation before starting — not an entry path
OSED is significantly harder than OSCP — plan for extended lab time
Portfolio (GitHub implants, CVEs, conference talks) matters more than certs in this space
High total cost — employer sponsorship for OSED recommended

prerequisites.experience: Red Team Operator experience required — CRTO or OSCP minimum. C, C#, or Go programming proficiency. Windows internals familiarity. This is a senior specialization, not an entry path.

cert_sequence.notes: White Knight Labs Offensive Development Practitioner Certification — the only performance-based offensive development cert. $500 on-demand, 48-hour practical exam + 48-hour professional report. Covers AMSI bypass, ETW patching, process injection variants, AV/EDR evasion, and implant development in C and Go. No MCQ — pass/fail on your working code and report quality. The most direct credential proof of offensive development capability available.

CWL Certified Windows Internals Red Team Operator — fills the Windows internals depth that pure offensive dev certs assume. Covers process injection, token manipulation, handle inheritance, and EDR blind spots via internals knowledge. Bridges tool-user and tool-builder at the OS level. Practical + report, 12+12 hours, $299.

OffSec Exploit Developer — Windows exploit development from buffer overflows to SEH, heap exploitation, and custom shellcode. The OffSec brand recognition is OSED's primary value for hiring managers. Significantly harder than OSCP — the 72-hour exam requires writing working exploits under time pressure. With ODPC completed you arrive at OSED with practical tooling context that makes the exploit dev content more relatable.

paths.name: Budget Track
paths.rationale: For self-funded practitioners building offensive development skills before committing to OSED's price. Zero2Automated adds malware RE depth, CBFRPro adds binary fuzzing and RE validation, ODPC is the capstone practical cert. Total cost under $1,000.
pros: Under $1,000 total — the most affordable offensive dev credential stack
Z2A + CBFRPro cover RE and fuzzing at depth
ODPC is the only hands-on offensive dev cert regardless of budget
cons: Lower HR name recognition than OSED
CBFRPro is a newer SecOps Group cert — building market presence
Portfolio remains essential — certs alone won't land senior offensive dev roles
No OffSec brand in the stack

prerequisites.experience: Red Team Operator experience required. Same baseline as standard track.

cert_sequence.notes: Zero2Automated — builds malware RE and analysis depth that informs better offensive tooling. Understanding how defenders analyze implants makes you a better implant developer. ~$390 lifetime, universally respected.

SecOps Group Certified Binary Fuzzing and Reversing Professional — AFL++, IDA/Ghidra workflow, memory-corruption identification. Adds formal RE and fuzzing credential at £250 (~$320). Practical hands-on exam.
ODPC capstone — the only performance-based offensive dev cert. With Z2A and CBFRPro foundations completed you arrive at ODPC with strong RE and analysis context. $500 on-demand.
